ariaSpec.usage: Announces the Menu orientation when vertical.
Indicates a popup menu is associated with the item.
Indicates whether the submenu is expanded.
Informs assistive technologies that a Menu item is disabled.
Expand arrow elements are hidden from assistive technologies.
The role of the nested menu displayed in a popup.
Scroll buttons are removed from the tab sequence.
Scroll buttons have an accessible label.
Points to the ID of the submenu. Only present when the item controls a submenu element.
Each nested menu has a deterministic id linked to the parent aria-controls.

kbSpec.behavior: (For root items) Opens the item and focuses the last child.; (For child items) Focuses the previous item.
(For root items) Opens the item and focuses the first child.; (For child items) Focuses the next item.
(For root items) Focuses the previous item.; (For child items) For direct children of a root item, focuses and opens the previous root item. Otherwise, closes and focuses the parent.
(For root items) Focuses the next item.; (For child items) If the item has children, opens the item and focuses the first child. Otherwise, focuses and opens the next root item.
Selects the focused item. If the item has children, opens the item and focuses the first child. If the item does not have children and has a URL, navigates to the specified URL.
Selects the focused item. If the item has children, opens the item and focuses the first child. If the item does not have children and has a URL, navigates to the specified URL.
(For child items) Closes and focuses the parent.
Focuses the next item with text starting with the character.
(For root items) opens the item and focuses the last child.; (For child items) closes and focuses the parent.
If the item has children, opens the item and focuses the first child. For child items without children, focuses and opens the next root item.
